violations.text: The beverage lowboy cooler in the front prep area was observed in poor repair (door/gasket not properly sealing when closed). Equipment components such as doors, seals, hinges, fasteners, and kick plates shall be kept intact, tight, and adjusted in accordance with manufacturer's specifications. New Violation. Correct By: 12/08/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the fan covers in both walk-in refrigerators have an accumulation of dust. New Violation. Correct By: 11/24/2025

The following was observed of the plumbing systems: 1) The food prep sink in the catering area was observed with a cup in-side the drain, creating an air-break. 2) The pipes draining from the dish machine and the pre-rinse area was observed with an air-break. New Violation. Correct By: 12/08/2025
The floors in the walk-in freezer has an accumulation of debris/food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 11/24/2025

The door to the outside, located in the back kitchen area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/06/2024
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was blocked by a trash can. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/21/2024

Egg whites were observed held at 130 degrees Fahrenheit. The item was observed to be double panned in the hot-holding unit. PIC instructed to remove double pans to allow for proper heating temperatures in the unit. PIC elected to dispose of item instead of reheating. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/21/2024

Various items in the first sandwich prep unit next to the beverage station was observed held at 44-45 degrees Fahrenheit. The prep unit was observed to be unplugged. The ambient temperature of the unit observed at 45 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Due to the unit being unplugged for an unknown amount of time, PIC voluntarily disposed of items. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/21/2024

The door to the outside, located in the back kitchen area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 03/06/2024
